channel.title: Taglines on Christian Mahnke
channel.description: Recent content in Taglines on Christian Mahnke

item.title: Citizen Art Historian
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description:
“Citizen Art Historian” is a mixture of “Citizen Scientist” and “Art Historian” and therefore more of a self-defined title. The definition could be: A non-expert who actively participates in the research, documentation, interpretation or preservation of art (history).

Examples of activities in this area are:

Blog posts on the topic:

  • Tagged with “Art”
  • Exhibition about Wilma Bräuner

Artist

  • Fritz Neumann aka Ric

Pages about architecture:

  • Never Built Göttingen
  • Brick Expressionism

item.title: Digital Humanities Communicator
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description:
A “Digital Humanities Communicator” is a science communicator specialising in digital humanities. This term is not made up, but is still relatively rare.

The following blog posts are examples of activities in this field:

  • HDR images for the visualisation of X-ray examinations of old paintings
  • Geodaten zur Identifikation einer Perspektive eines Holzschnittes
  • Uranium glass in 3D - Ultra violet photogrammetry

item.title: Creative Technologist
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description:
“Creative Technologist” refers to a person who either works at the interface of technology and design or is generally entrusted with the creative use of technology. This role involves developing creative (web) applications, prototypes, experiments and corresponding concepts…

Examples of activities in this field are

Blog posts from the field:

  • Visualisation AI segmentation with HDR
  • Haptic feedback for digitised fabrics - IIIF annotations with vibration
  • A “glowing” wallpaper - HDR IIIF
  • An AI reads out an old letter template - The letter itself (in German)
  • Visualisation of a 70s video game - Pong
  • Making digital collection objects exhibition-ready through the use of computer vision methods

Pages with innovative forms of presentation:

  • VintageReality (in German) - preparation of stereograms for, among other things, different coloured glasses, Google Cardboard, as a wobble image and extraction of depth information. Details in this blog post - Use of computer vision methods

item.title: Enthusiast & Collector
item.author: mail@christianmahnke.de (Christian Mahnke)
item.description:
The original self-designation. I can also be found under the latter in museum-digital(https://term.museum-digital.de/md-de/persinst/322830).

It describes the original scope of my websites, but since the scope has grown considerably in the meantime, there are now several titles.

Examples of activities in this area are:

Blog posts on the topic:

  • Donations
  • Unusual objects

Sonstige Seiten

  • Projektemacher: Pages and Blogs
